reviews.review_msg: Midterm was hard because of time constraints, but assignments were a free 100% and the final was pretty easy other than having to do like 500 manual calculations. Ken is whatever. Class is completely unnecessary because he just reads off slides. Just understand all the examples and relevant exercises in the textbook and it's pretty chill.

Ken Peng's assignments, tutorials, and exams were very helpful in learning the course material. Stat 445 is hard if you haven't taken any machine learning and linear algebra. He was quite generous with his grading scheme and was accessible outside of class. As long as you read the textbook and attend tutorials, the class is chill.

Ken reads off slides and writes illegibly on the screen sometimes. He is barely audible in lecture. His exams ask about things that you never cover in the assignments or tutorials and he doesn't give you enough time to complete them. Exams are more linear algebra than statistics. The worst instructor I've had in over 120 credits at SFU.
